meta.title: Domenico Modugno Statue | Polignano a Mare
meta.description: Complete guide to the Domenico Modugno Statue in Polignano a Mare, Puglia. Hours, directions, photos and reviews.

hero.title: Domenico Modugno Statue
hero.subtitle: The most iconic viewpoint in Polignano a Mare

intro.title: Discover the Statue
intro.description: Located on the waterfront of Polignano a Mare, this bronze monument honors Domenico Modugno — the legendary Italian singer-songwriter who captivated the world with 'Volare' (Nel blu dipinto di blu). The statue depicts him with arms outstretched toward the Adriatic Sea, a gesture that has become the symbol of the city.

highlights.title: Highlights
items: Photo icon — the most instagrammable spot in Polignano a Mare
Free access 24 hours a day, 365 days a year
Breathtaking views of the cliffs and the Adriatic Sea
Just a short walk from the historic center and the famous Lama Monachile beach
Tribute to the singer of 'Volare', Italian musical heritage

sections.title: Monumento a Domenico Modugno
sections.content: Created by sculptor Hermann Mejer and unveiled on May 31, 2009, this bronze monument is more than just an artwork; it is a tribute from the locals to a great artist. Standing about 3 meters tall, the statue vividly captures a moment of Domenico Modugno's passionate performance on stage.

sections.title: Modugno Statue & The Volare Statue
sections.content: Among international tourists, it is often referred to as the 'Modugno Statue' or 'The Volare Statue'. Facing the sea with arms wide open, the statue symbolizes an embrace of the world and a desire to fly freely, perfectly illustrating the mood of the song that drove the world crazy.

sections.title: Polignano a Mare
sections.content: Polignano a Mare is a pearl of the Apulia region in Italy, famous for its white houses built on limestone cliffs and the crystal-clear Adriatic Sea. As the birthplace of Domenico Modugno, this city gave him endless inspiration, and today this statue has become the city's most shining cultural calling card.

sections.title: The Legend of 'Volare'
sections.content: 'Volare' (originally titled 'Nel blu, dipinto di blu'), released in 1958, swept the globe and won two Grammy Awards. The song is not only a milestone in Italian music history but also a global symbol of Italian optimism, romance, and passion. Standing in front of the statue with the sea breeze blowing, one can almost hear that familiar melody echoing.

reviews.title: Visitor Reviews
reviews.declaration: Ratings and reviews from Google Maps (last updated: 2026). We show only a selection of verified high-rated reviews. To see all complete and most recent reviews, click the link below.
reviews.moreReviews: See all reviews on Google Maps

items.name: Marco Bianchi
items.text: Wonderful place! The statue is beautiful and the sea view is spectacular. Perfect for sunset photos.

items.name: Laura Rossi
items.text: A magical place. I sang Volare in front of the statue, pure emotion. Not to be missed!

items.name: Giovanni De Luca
items.text: Symbol of Polignano. The cliff position is incredible, especially at sunset.

items.name: Sofia Moretti
items.text: Beautiful monument with stunning views. I recommend visiting both during the day and at sunset.

items.name: Alessandro Ferretti
items.text: A must-visit! The statue is imposing and the panorama is one of the most beautiful in Puglia.

items.name: Chiara Romano
items.text: Very atmospheric. Can be crowded in summer, better to visit early morning.

items.name: Francesco Esposito
items.text: A perfect tribute to Modugno. The sea breeze and view make this place unforgettable.

items.name: Elena Colombo
items.text: I opened my arms like the statue and felt like I was flying. A unique experience!
